messages.author: alaspooryorick
messages.text: this thread will cover my work and the process of integrating support for both usb and wifi or ble connection methods in a single image, reducing the concurrent images for the heltec v3 to 2.
messages.content_html: this thread will cover my work and the process of integrating support for both usb and wifi or ble connection methods in a single image, reducing the concurrent images for the heltec v3 to 2.

4
1

messages.author: alaspooryorick
messages.text: Concerns for project acceptance: 1. Image size - likely a non-issue, as all esp32 devices should have enough storage for the increased library count 2. Power consumption - possibly an issue, but the ability to customize and toggle what's enabled would likely nullify. 3. Defaults for esp32 devices without buttons- configuration page changes necessary, or CLI enable/disable of features 4. The usability of this seems limited- if I read messages from my device on my phone, they're deleted and wouldn't be visible via usb. (don't delete messages until queue is full?) 5. hey an integrated approach to serial is cool and all but I like my purpose built images - Idk, then haha (edited)
messages.content_html: Concerns for project acceptance: 1. Image size - likely a non-issue, as all esp32 devices should have enough storage for the increased library count 2. Power consumption - possibly an issue, but the ability to customize and toggle what's enabled would likely nullify. 3. Defaults for esp32 devices without buttons- configuration page changes necessary, or CLI enable/disable of features 4. The usability of this seems limited- if I read messages from my device on my phone, they're deleted and wouldn't be visible via usb. (don't delete messages until queue is full?) 5. hey an integrated approach to serial is cool and all but I like my purpose built images - Idk, then haha (edited)

messages.author: alaspooryorick
messages.text: In the far future, I want to review the ble/wifi driver to see if dual-stack support is possible/feasible. I know it exists, but it's possible battery and performance impacts may outweigh the slight convenience. Even if they're note side-by side, I might see about a toggle to enable either or so only 1 firmware excists
messages.content_html: In the far future, I want to review the ble/wifi driver to see if dual-stack support is possible/feasible. I know it exists, but it's possible battery and performance impacts may outweigh the slight convenience. Even if they're note side-by side, I might see about a toggle to enable either or so only 1 firmware excists

messages.author: Tom
messages.text: This would be a great improvment to Meshcore, in line with how Meshtastic appears to work, and minimise the need to have different firmware versions, importantly enabling Wi-Fi access for people. Would you be doing this by adding Wi-Fi credentials into a configuration, or would you still want people to hardcode and compile the SSID?
messages.content_html: This would be a great improvment to Meshcore, in line with how Meshtastic appears to work, and minimise the need to have different firmware versions, importantly enabling Wi-Fi access for people. Would you be doing this by adding Wi-Fi credentials into a configuration, or would you still want people to hardcode and compile the SSID?
